sections.paragraph:
ἄριστον, ου, τό (s. ἀριστάω) in early Gk. meal eaten early in the day (later called ἀκράτισμα, from the custom of dipping bread in wine), then the noon meal.

① breakfast (so Hom. et al.; GDI 5495, 45 [Ionic]; POxy 519, 17; 736, 28; PTebt 116, 36; Sus 13 Theod., cp. 12 LXX) Lk 14:12 differentiated fr. δεῖπνον (as Polyaenus 4, 3, 32 ἄριστον κ. δεῖπνον; Jos., Ant. 8, 356).

② noon meal (Thu. 4, 90, 3; 7, 81, 1; Athen. 1, 9, 10, 11b δεῖπνον μεσημβρινόν, cp. 2 Km 24:15; Tob 2:1; JosAs 3:3; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 14 Jac.; Jos., Ant. 5, 190) Mt 22:4 and meal gener. (PTebt 120, 82 [I b.c.]; Tob 12:13; Bel 34; 37; ViHab 7 [p. 86, 10 Sch.]; Jos., Ant. 2, 2) Lk 11:38; 14:15 v.l.—In both mngs. loanw. in rabb.—B. 354. DELG. M-M.

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. fr. = from

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

gener. gener. = generally

ViHab ViHab , s. ViIs

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 131
